headline: Trump's 'Massive Armada' Closes In On Iran
Silver Plunges More Than 30%, and Analysts See More Pain Ahead
summary: The price of silver plummeted by over 30% on Friday, marking a historic drop. This drastic fall was attributed to a strengthening US dollar and investor reactions to President Trump's Fed pick.

summary: Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) experienced a significant drop, falling below the $80,000 mark on Saturday.

headline: Diaz-Canel: Trump Aims to 'Suffocate' Cuba With Oil Tariff Threat
summary: Cuba's President Miguel Diaz-Canel condemned Donald Trump's oil-linked tariff threat as an effort to choke Cuba's economy amid worsening fuel shortages and blackouts.

headline: Navy Taps L3Harris Red Wolf for Long-Range Strikes
summary: L3Harris won a U.S. Navy deal to develop Red Wolf long-range strike vehicles for Marines, while reaffirming higher 2026 sales guidance.

headline: Affirm Expands Exclusive Partnership Across Expedia Brands
summary: Affirm will serve as Expedia's exclusive BNPL provider across key U.S. brands, offering instant approvals and installment plans up to 24 months, with a Canada rollout next.

headline: Investors Pull Nearly $818 Million From Bitcoin ETFs as Cryptocurrency Tanks
summary: Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) exchange-traded funds (ETFs) experienced a substantial one-day reversal last week.

headline: Demis Hassabis Tells Undergrads To Get 'Unbelievably Proficient' With AI Tools, Google DeepMind CEO Calls It Better Bet Than Traditional Internships
summary: AI tools may be a better bet for students than traditional internships. AI is disrupting junior roles as companies automate tasks. Technical fluency is a strong signal for employers, Demis Hassabis suggests.

headline: Jesse Itzler Once Name-Dropped Matt Damon To Coca-Cola's CEO And Even Got The Actor To Climb A Coconut Tree, Then He Landed The Deal
summary: Jesse Itzler shared how a stalled Coca-Cola pitch turned into Zico Coconut Water's acquisition after he name-dropped Matt Damon and sent a viral coconut-tree video, capping a career that spans billion-dollar exits, music, authorship and entrepreneurship.

headline: Nvidia Poised To Make Record Investment In OpenAI's Current Funding Round
summary: Nvidia is preparing to make a substantial investment in OpenAI's ongoing funding round, as confirmed by CEO Jensen Huang.

Tesla's Q4 Earnings Call Gave Us An Insight Into Elon Musk's Optimus Ambitions, As Well As Challenges
summary: Tesla's Q4 earnings revealed plans for a million-unit Optimus factory, as well as next-generation Optimus capable of learning by observation.

headline: Wintermute: "Everything Becomes Tradeable" In 2026—Prediction Markets To Replace Insurance
summary: Wintermute sees crypto as the internet's clearing layer in 2026, with prediction markets replacing insurance and stablecoins fixing settlement.

summary: Cyber Hornet expands its ETF lineup with S&P 500 and crypto strategies, giving investors measured exposure to Ethereum, Solana and XRP.

headline: Marc Andreessen Says AI May Save The Global Economy As Population Growth Slows: 'Human Workers Are Going To Be At A Premium'
summary: Marc Andreessen says AI could help offset economic challenges from declining populations while boosting growth and innovation.

headline: TSMC Founder Morris Chang Makes First Public Appearance In A Year, Meets Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang For Dinner In Taipei
summary: TSMC founder Morris Chang ended a year-long public absence with a private Taipei dinner alongside Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang, reassuring the semiconductor industry of his sharpness and underscoring TSMC's continued central role as global chip capacity expands worldwide.

headline: Russia's Lukoil Reaches Tentative Deal To Sell Most Overseas Assets To US Private Equity Firm Carlyle Amid Sanctions
summary: Lukoil has agreed to sell most of its international assets to Carlyle amid Western sanctions and competing bids.

headline: Amazon Seeks More Time From FCC For Starlink Rival Leo Satellite Rollout Amid Rocket Shortages
summary: Amazon.com is asking the FCC for a two-year extension on its 1,600-satellite deployment deadline for its $10 billion Amazon Leo internet network, citing rocket shortages and launch delays, as it aims to catch up with SpaceX's Starlink while warning that denying the extension could slow U.S. broadband expansion.

headline: Nvidia's $100 Billion OpenAI Investment Plan Reportedly Hits Snag Amid Internal Concerns
summary: Nvidia's proposed $100 billion investment in OpenAI has reportedly stalled as CEO Jensen Huang raises concerns over the AI startup's spending discipline and intensifying competition, prompting both companies to reconsider a smaller, nonbinding equity deal even as OpenAI attracts heavy Big Tech interest and posts surging revenue.

headline: Reid Hoffman Urges Silicon Valley To Speak Out Against Trump's Policies: 'We Can't Bend The Knee'
summary: LinkedIn co-founder Reid Hoffman urges Silicon Valley leaders to speak out against ICE killings, calling inaction unacceptable.

headline: Disney CEO Bob Iger To Reportedly Step Down Before Contract Ends, Board To Vote On Successor Next Week As Investors Await Q1 Results
summary: Walt Disney (NYSE: DIS) CEO Bob Iger has reportedly told associates he plans to step down and pull back from daily management before his contract expires on Dec. 31, and the entertainment giant's board is set to meet next week to vote on a successor.

headline: Eli Lilly CEO Says Medicare Coverage Of Obesity Drugs Could Be Game-Changer For Its Weight-Loss Pill
summary: Eli Lilly CEO Dave Ricks said upcoming Medicare coverage of obesity drugs could be a game-changer for the launch of its experimental weight-loss pill orforglipron, potentially expanding access to millions of patients even as competition with Novo Nordisk intensifies ahead of both companies' earnings.

headline: Trump Picks Brett Matsumoto To Lead Bureau Of Labor Statistics, Says He's Confident The Veteran Economist Will 'Quickly Fix' Issues
summary: Trump taps veteran economist Brett Matsumoto to lead the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) after abruptly firing the agency's commissioner in August.

headline: Jeff Bezos-Backed Blue Origin Halts New Shepard Flights For Two Years, Shifts Focus To Lunar Program
summary: Blue Origin pauses New Shepard suborbital flights to focus on lunar lander development for NASA's Artemis program.

headline: Ex-Google Engineer Faces Decades In Prison After Jury Finds He Stole AI Secrets For China
summary: A federal jury convicted former Google engineer Linwei Ding of stealing AI chip and supercomputer trade secrets to benefit China in the first U.S. AI economic espionage case, exposing him to decades in prison.

headline: Brief US Government Shutdown Likely Despite Senate Deal— Can Prediction Markets Handle Gray Areas?
summary: The U.S. Senate late Friday approved a bipartisan government funding package aimed at preventing a prolonged shutdown, but a brief lapse in funding now appears unavoidable as the House of Representatives is not expected to vote until Monday.

headline: Elon Musk's SpaceX Profits Topped $8 Billion Last Year As Record-Setting IPO Talk Heats Up: Report
summary: SpaceX reportedly earned $8 billion on $15 billion to $16 billion in revenue last year as banks eye a $50 billion IPO at a valuation above $1.5 trillion.

headline: DeepSeek Turns Nvidia Customer As China Approves H200 Chip Purchases: Report
summary: China has conditionally approved DeepSeek and other major tech firms to buy Nvidia's H200 chips, undercutting earlier fears that the low-cost AI startup would reduce demand for Nvidia's high-end processors.
